jokes.text: What rhymes with majority? Nothing. Come on, you still working on that funny song about the new Speaker? - Give it a break. - Oh, priority. And authority. Sorority. Oh, my God, there's a million words that rhyme with it.
jokes.setup: Character claims nothing rhymes with majority
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as multiple rhymes are rattled off
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep setup-punchline with immediate deflation. Well-structured but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Look where Jonah's coming from. Oh, that grin, the keys. Oh, God. He's been given West Exec parking. Yeah, the holy grail of parking.
jokes.setup: Characters spot Jonah approaching with keys and a grin
jokes.punchline: The revelation that parking is the 'holy grail' in this world
jokes.explanation: Perfect Veep worldbuilding - showing how petty status symbols matter in DC. Character-specific and well-observed.

jokes.text: Good morning, gentlemen. What a delightful day. I was gonna walk to work, but then... - You got West Exec? - It's just a parking spot, Dan. To be jealous of that, you'd have to be a shallow kind of... I don't know, dick, I guess.
jokes.setup: Jonah smugly downplays the parking spot's importance
jokes.punchline: His casual insult revealing his own smugness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah character moment - simultaneously dismissive and gloating, with typical crude delivery.

jokes.text: Look at the size of his coffee mug. Jeez, you could hide a baby in that thing.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Ben's oversized coffee mug in photo
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison to hiding a baby in it
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy with absurd escalation. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: You look concerned, but steely. - Yeah. - Like a lady soldier.
jokes.setup: Gary complimenting Selina's appearance in the photo
jokes.punchline: The awkward 'lady soldier' descriptor
jokes.explanation: Gary's characteristic awkward flattery attempt. Character-specific but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Who the hell is religious as a teenager? I was. I mean, smoke some weed, for Christ's sake, right?
jokes.punchline: Gary's earnest admission followed by Selina's profane advice
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast and mild hypocrisy with the casual profanity.

jokes.text: Oh, there are not gonna be any veterans at this teen prayer breakfast thing tomorrow, are there? / No, ma'am. No missing legs.
jokes.setup: Selina's concern about veterans at a teen event
jokes.punchline: Gary's blunt confirmation focusing on the specific visual of missing limbs
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about political image management. Both characters' casual callousness toward veterans reveals their shallow priorities.

jokes.text: Oh, God, look at the president. - Jeez, he looks so... - He looks jowly. Ah, look at those cheeks. It's like two little clutch bags attached to his face.
jokes.setup: Looking at unflattering photo of the president
jokes.punchline: Specific clutch bag comparison for his jowls
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual metaphor that's specific, unexpected, and quotable. Strong craft.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, with the bags. It's always bags with you. Got to get out of the bag zone. Come on, Gary.
jokes.setup: Selina recognizing Gary's pattern of bag comparisons
jokes.punchline: 'Get out of the bag zone' as relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific running gag establishment. 'Bag zone' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Would it be okay if I showed you some ideas I have regarding strategy? No time, Mr. Egan. They're mostly Flash based, but I put them on YouTube. I just said I had no time.
jokes.punchline: Jonah completely ignoring the dismissal and continuing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah obliviousness. Kent's exasperated repetition lands well.

jokes.text: We're all being hit by deadlines, right, Mike? Yes, sir. They can swing around and smack you like a boom.
jokes.punchline: Mike's awkward sailing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mike's sailing obsession. Mildly awkward but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Nothing fancy, but she gets the job done. You know, like a cast-iron skillet or Kevin Bacon.
jokes.setup: Mike describing his boat as functional
jokes.punchline: Random Kevin Bacon comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd non-sequitur. Kevin Bacon comparison is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: That's real man's sailing. Leaning out in the tack, your big man balls dipping in the salty sea.
jokes.punchline: The crude 'big man balls' imagery
jokes.explanation: Kent's rare moment of crude enthusiasm is character-revealing and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I have no children and I wish for none, but she's my family.
jokes.punchline: The stark declaration about not wanting children followed by boat-as-family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent characterization - robotic yet oddly touching. The stark child-hatred followed by boat love is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: You know, I once went powerboating on Lake Erie. Amazing rush.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to join the sailing conversation
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point with powerboating
jokes.explanation: Dan's typical oblivious attempt to join in. Sets up the sailing vs powerboating distinction.

jokes.text: You don't talk powerboating to us sailors, Dan. What's the fucking difference?
jokes.punchline: Dan's frustrated profane response
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the sailing setup. Dan's exasperation feels earned.

jokes.text: Jonah, out. Or else I'll see if my pepper spray works.
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact threat to test pepper spray on him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - her deadpan delivery of a threat that's both specific and casual. Shows everyone's attitude toward Jonah.

jokes.text: You've already bugged the shit out of everyone else in the building? I'm sorry, what? Say that again. Jonah, out. Or else I'll see if my pepper spray works.
jokes.punchline: Pepper spray threat escalation
jokes.explanation: Good Amy hostility moment with unexpected pepper spray escalation.

jokes.text: 13,000. No, 14,500. Is this the amount of times you've admired yourself in the mirror today? No, I'm guessing that's the difference in salary between this job and the one that you just interviewed for.
jokes.punchline: Amy's precise salary knowledge reveal
jokes.explanation: Well-structured reversal. Amy's detective skills showcase is satisfying.

jokes.text: Simple makeup, higher neckline. Flats don't go with that dress, which means you probably have heels in your bag. Coffee from corner bakery implying you were at one of the lobbying shops on 18th.
jokes.punchline: The Sherlock Holmes-level detail observation
jokes.explanation: Good Dan character moment showing his observational skills and smugness.

jokes.text: I think he'll be pulling Bon Jovi out of a burning tank in case we all forgot what a fucking war hero he is.
jokes.punchline: Absurd Bon Jovi rescue scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation. The Bon Jovi detail makes it memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: It looks like I'm tweeting when a guy loses his leg. Yeah, it does, ma'am.
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Amy's characteristic blunt honesty.

jokes.text: This is Kent's doing. He's trying to screw me. I'm gonna go stab him. I'm stabbing him.
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to stabbing threats
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina violent hyperbole. Functional but not special.

jokes.text: I'll start running the acid bath.
jokes.punchline: Gary's casual acid bath offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - cheerfully helpful with something horrifying. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: This is the photograph? No, just wondering if you think this sweater goes with the dress.
jokes.setup: Kent expecting to discuss the photo controversy
jokes.punchline: Selina's fashion redirect
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Selina's priorities. Well-executed but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That's how he looks. That's his face. Why didn't you just take the good one of me, Photoshop it in? I can't airbrush history, ma'am. I'm not Joseph Stalin.
jokes.punchline: Kent's Stalin comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kent moment. The Stalin reference is both historically apt and character-revealing.

jokes.text: No, that's true. You don't have a tenth of his charm.
jokes.punchline: Selina saying Stalin had more charm
jokes.explanation: Brutal and perfectly crafted comeback. Comparing Kent unfavorably to Stalin is peak Selina cruelty.

jokes.text: Oh, so now I have to have Dan and Mike, Rodgers and Hammershit, come up with a new song in an afternoon?
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about needing a new song
jokes.punchline: 'Hammershit' pun on Hammerstein
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but relies on simple profanity substitution. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's a thing women can do, like smelling nice and wrapping gifts.
jokes.punchline: Sexist stereotypical examples
jokes.explanation: Gary's characteristic oblivious sexism. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What are you laughing about, Jolly Green Jizzface?
jokes.punchline: Crude Jolly Green Giant pun
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina crude insult. The alliteration makes it memorable despite being lowbrow.

jokes.text: I'm a meme ma'am? What are you talking about? Speak English, boy.
jokes.punchline: Selina's confusion and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor. Selina's dismissive 'boy' is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If there was a tsunami, you'd be genuinely looking at your phone 'cause you'd be checking the weather.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Selina in disaster memes
jokes.punchline: Gary's logical defense of phone-checking during tsunami
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary logic - technically correct but missing the point entirely.

jokes.text: No, it's just a meme, ma'am. Not a meme ma'am. And usually they flame out after about 48 hours, but sometimes they blow up and become a super meme like Downfall or Gangnam.
jokes.punchline: The pedantic correction followed by earnest explanation
jokes.explanation: Jonah's characteristic pedantry and internet expertise. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Take all these meaningless syllables with you and just get out.
jokes.punchline: Reducing all tech terms to 'meaningless syllables'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Selina dismissal. 'Meaningless syllables' perfectly captures her tech frustration.

jokes.text: Okay, yeah, that's a cherry on top of this whole turd cake.
jokes.setup: Jonah mentioning he'll be at the dinner
jokes.punchline: Turd cake with cherry metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good mixed metaphor that's both disgusting and quotable. Classic Selina imagery.

jokes.text: That would have made people piss in their pants.
jokes.punchline: Overstating the comedy with pants-pissing
jokes.explanation: Weak crude humor. Mike's overconfidence is character-appropriate but not clever.

jokes.text: That's not a rhyme. You're just matching a word with the same word.
jokes.punchline: Dan's accurate but pedantic correction
jokes.explanation: Good Dan pedantry moment. The correction is both accurate and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Comedy songs are the mark of a douchebag. You're wrong. You know why? Three words... Weird Al Yankovic. Shit Al Yankoshit.
jokes.setup: Debate about comedy songs and Weird Al defense
jokes.punchline: Dan's crude Weird Al pun
jokes.explanation: Simple profanity substitution pun. Character conflict is good but joke is lazy.

jokes.text: He's half robot, half robot. - He's a robot.
jokes.setup: Dan describing Kent as part robot
jokes.punchline: Mike's logical correction that he's just full robot
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic correction. The redundancy is funny and Mike's correction lands well.

jokes.text: If the first one bombs, people are gonna be sitting out there thinking, 'Shit the bed, we got 49 more of these to go.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of doing a '50 Ways' parody song
jokes.punchline: Amy's vivid description of audience dread at the prospect of 49 more verses
jokes.explanation: Excellent practical comedy insight - Amy imagining the audience's horror at a lengthy parody song format. Shows her pragmatic perspective.

jokes.text: There was this one girl, smart, who had no interest in me. She wasn't gay either. I checked.
jokes.setup: Dan telling story about college rejection
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of 'checking' her sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good Dan character moment showing his clueless persistence and casual boundary crossing.

jokes.text: In college there was only one girl that was interested in me. And it turns out she was actually a lesbian.
jokes.punchline: The girl being lesbian despite being interested in him
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Mike moment. The logic puzzle of lesbian interest is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Because he'd be happy if I shot him in the face. He would be. I've actually thought about it.
jokes.setup: Discussing Gary's happiness with his treatment
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual admission of murder fantasies
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina dark humor with perfect casual delivery of horrifying thoughts.

jokes.text: That's like half of what he should have, right?
jokes.punchline: Selina's matter-of-fact math about limb loss
jokes.explanation: Characteristically insensitive Selina observation delivered with perfect casualness.

jokes.text: Just bought Sue for a dollar. Who's next?
jokes.punchline: Reducing employee retention to commodity purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina transactional view of relationships. Economical and character-revealing.

jokes.text: I've never eaten with Gary before. I've eaten next to him, but...
jokes.punchline: The distinction between eating 'with' vs 'next to' someone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight showing Selina's emotional distance. The distinction is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: Yeah, we hated him long before it was even fashionable to hate him.
jokes.punchline: Making Jonah-hatred into a hipster trend
jokes.explanation: Good hipster parody applied to office dynamics. Well-crafted and quotable.

jokes.text: This is like a wildlife documentary. Jonah in his natural habitat. He might defecate in a bush.
jokes.punchline: Wildlife documentary comparison with defecation threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent nature documentary parody with perfect escalation to scatological humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's just feeling bad. He's... you know, he's sugary. - Yeah. - Or he's not sugary enough.
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong 'sugary' symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah medical ignorance. 'Sugary' as a symptom is both wrong and funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's got fat wrists. You know?
jokes.punchline: Fat wrists as a diabetes symptom
jokes.explanation: 'Fat wrists' is brilliantly specific and medically absurd. Peak Jonah invention under pressure.

jokes.text: You're like an old VCR, but with a bigger mouth.
jokes.punchline: VCR comparison with mouth size escalation
jokes.explanation: Good technology insult with specific physical escalation. Well-crafted Dan meanness.

jokes.text: You've been following me like Nancy Drew and his butt-sniffing dog
jokes.punchline: Nancy Drew gender confusion and dog detail
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah confusion with Nancy Drew gender and random dog addition.

jokes.text: But at least when I'm crying about it, I am crying about it in my car in West Wing Exec parking.
jokes.setup: Jonah admitting he has nothing to do
jokes.punchline: At least he cries in prestigious parking
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment. Jonah's priorities are beautifully skewed - parking status over actual purpose.

jokes.text: I actually wrote it in my diary and stared at it for a full five minutes.
jokes.setup: Gary thanking Selina for dinner invitation
jokes.punchline: The diary detail and staring duration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary overshare. The specific five minutes detail makes it beautifully pathetic.

jokes.text: ♪ Can Dana come along? ♪ - Oh, really? - No. That was a bad idea.
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and backtracking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Gary's immediate recognition of his mistake. The singing makes it worse.

jokes.text: Now, am I getting older or are you getting younger? We're both getting older. That's the third option, of course. That's great stuff.
jokes.punchline: Amy's literal correction and Jim's awkward recovery
jokes.explanation: Good awkward politician flirtation with Amy's characteristic directness deflating it.

jokes.text: Well, they seemed very excited about the possibility of a woman becoming president. Not necessarily you. ( Laughs )
jokes.setup: Exchange about young voters and veterans vs women
jokes.punchline: The 'not necessarily you' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Perfect political insult callback. The structure mirrors his earlier comment for maximum impact.

jokes.text: Amy, you should stop eating so much. What the fuck are you talking about? You're stress eating.
jokes.punchline: Amy's outraged response
jokes.explanation: Good Selina boundary-crossing with Amy's characteristic profane pushback.

jokes.text: I mean, I hope I don't have a cat because, believe me, that poor fucker's dead.
jokes.punchline: Imaginary dead cat from neglect
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about work-life balance. The casual 'poor fucker' makes it memorably harsh.

jokes.text: I wouldn't want you to become a fat, neurotic freak. Speaking as a friend.
jokes.punchline: 'Speaking as a friend' qualifier after harsh insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina 'friendship' - brutal honesty disguised as care. The qualifier makes it worse.

jokes.text: Chung's guy had a face like a Christmas ham.
jokes.punchline: Christmas ham face description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and disturbing visual comparison. Christmas ham for a burn victim is memorably awful.

jokes.text: That isn't impressive at all. - He's just spitting, right?
jokes.punchline: Reducing beatboxing to just spitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect dismissive Selina observation. Reducing a skill to its base components is characteristic.

jokes.text: Of course, all she had to do was unlock her bedroom door, but thanks anyway.
jokes.setup: Praising Selina's role in freeing hostages
jokes.punchline: Implying the hostages were sexual partners
jokes.explanation: Good political roast joke structure with sexual implications. Well-timed and economical.

jokes.text: You just need to keep in mind that I'm not Selina Dion.
jokes.punchline: Celine Dion pun with her own name
jokes.explanation: Decent name pun showing self-awareness. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This is imbecilic. You're right. It's asinine. No, keep smiling, Mr. Ryan. I'm smiling falsely. So should you.
jokes.setup: Kent and Jim watching Selina's song performance
jokes.punchline: Instructions for fake smiling while insulting the performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect political fakeness moment. Kent's robotic instruction about false smiling is character-perfect.

jokes.text: You killed better than Chung killed and he's actually killed.
jokes.punchline: Performance 'killing' vs actual wartime killing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay contrasting performance success with literal killing. Peak Amy wit.

jokes.text: I had to pretend not to like it to Kent while pretending to like it like he was pretending to like it, but he didn't actually like it and I actually really liked it.
jokes.punchline: The convoluted layers of pretending
jokes.explanation: Perfect political doublethink maze. The structure gets complex but captures the absurdity well.

jokes.text: Ma'am, your song last night made my lips curl. - Uh... ( laughs ) - Upwards.
jokes.punchline: Clarification that it was positive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent robotic attempt at compliments. The clarification makes it beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: Uh, no, you don't want me, sir. You do want Dan. - He's great. - I've got a big bounce coming on. I can feel it. I'm like you.
jokes.punchline: 'Big bounce' comment with unfortunate comparison
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo that works but isn't exceptional. Mike's desperation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ma'am. Ma'am, please don't let him take me. I don't want to go to numbers camp. / I can't help you. Just let go. / Please. Please. Let go of me, all right?
jokes.setup: Mike's desperate plea to avoid working with Kent
jokes.punchline: His pathetic 'numbers camp' description and physical clinging
jokes.explanation: 'Numbers camp' perfectly captures Mike's fear of Kent's analytical world. Physical comedy of him clinging to Selina adds to the desperation.

jokes.text: I don't make impressions, ma'am. I've literally lived with people who insist I was never there.
jokes.setup: Mike denying he made an impression on Kent
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of being forgettable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike self-deprecation taken to absurd extreme. The roommate detail makes it memorably pathetic.

jokes.text: I become Mike, you become an ugly me.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining the job swap arrangement
jokes.punchline: Describing Jonah as ugly version of himself
jokes.explanation: Good economical insult. Mike calling himself the better-looking version of Jonah is perfectly backhanded.

jokes.text: Isn't that great? Would you ever describe a cheese as dusky? Not in a million years, no. I wouldn't do that.
jokes.punchline: Selina's blunt rejection of pretentious cheese vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between pretentious food talk and Selina's directness. Character-appropriate response.

jokes.text: And they would have made her an offer that she couldn't refuse. Actually, the mafia involvement in the dairy industry is minimal.
jokes.setup: Selina making Godfather reference about Italian cheese
jokes.punchline: Dana's literal correction about mafia dairy involvement
jokes.explanation: Dana's literal-mindedness kills Selina's joke attempt. Character contrast works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: Think about it. In 'The Godfather,' no cheese. - Yeah, you're right. - You know I'm right. - Boom! - That's her catchphrase.
jokes.setup: Dana proving her point about Godfather/cheese
jokes.punchline: Gary identifying 'Boom' as Dana's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Gary's helpful catchphrase explanation is endearing but not particularly funny. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: A Lev... a new Leviathan! Isn't that great? Does it have the same layout, honey? Because it took you so long to learn the other one.
jokes.setup: Gary's excitement about new bag gift
jokes.punchline: Dana's practical concern about learning new layout
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic showing Dana's practical nature deflating Gary's excitement.

jokes.text: ♪ I love my new bag! ♪ I can just sew some new pockets in. That's okay.
jokes.setup: Gary's musical expression of bag love
jokes.punchline: Immediate practical concern about pocket modifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment combining his enthusiasms with immediate practical concerns. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: You know, while you're getting to know your own mind, I'm getting to know the rest of you. - ( Laughs ) - And Mama likes. - Excuse me. - Mama like it.
jokes.setup: Dana making sexual advance on Gary during crisis
jokes.punchline: The 'Mama likes' comments during inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Awkward sexual timing during crisis. The 'Mama likes' repetition is cringe but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I won't touch anything. Oops, I just started a war with Iran. - Joke. - That's not even remotely funny.
jokes.setup: Dana joking about accidentally starting war
jokes.punchline: Selina's harsh rejection of the attempt
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about bad political humor. Selina's dismissal is the real punchline.

jokes.text: Well, our European visit just got turned into a one-way trip to hell and back. - That's actually a return. - You know what? Could you just shut it just for like two seconds?
jokes.setup: Selina's dramatic description of European trip
jokes.punchline: Dana's logical correction about round-trip implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dana literal-mindedness ruining Selina's dramatic moment. The logic is annoyingly correct.

jokes.text: I think it's pronounced Georges Denis. I'm not a hick. Screw you, Depardieu.
jokes.setup: Reading French criticism and Amy's pronunciation correction
jokes.punchline: Selina's misdirected anger at wrong French person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's cultural ignorance. Yelling at random French celebrity is perfectly Selina.

jokes.text: I just liked the song so much. And I thought you wanted it out there, so I just put it out there.
jokes.setup: Discovering Jonah caused the European crisis
jokes.punchline: Jonah's innocent explanation of massive diplomatic error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - well-meaning action causing international incident.

jokes.text: I just liked the song so much. And I thought you wanted it out there, so I just put it out there.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining why he posted the offensive song
jokes.punchline: His complete obliviousness to consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah oblivious helpfulness causing disaster. Character-consistent destructive incompetence.

jokes.text: Get off the plane. What? - Get off of the plane. - Ma'am, we're taxiing. I don't give a shit. Get the fuck off my plane.
jokes.punchline: Demanding plane ejection while taxiing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's rage. The impracticality of mid-taxi ejection adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: But I didn't even own a car. I just had to sign a two-year lease for the Nissan Cube.
jokes.punchline: He leased a car specifically for the parking spot
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and ironic consequences. The Nissan Cube detail makes it perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: He should really be on suicide watch. Yeah, make sure he goes through with it.
jokes.punchline: Selina's encouragement of suicide completion
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina darkness. Gary's genuine concern contrasted with Selina's casual cruelty is perfect.

jokes.text: I leased a Nissan Cube from you the other day. What are my options if I don't need it anymore? Can I just give that back? No? ( Sighs ) Paper.
jokes.setup: Jonah dealing with car lease consequences
jokes.punchline: His defeated acceptance and paper request
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing consequences continuing to unfold. The defeated 'Paper' is perfectly pathetic.
